description_long: This chaise features a modern, minimalist design with a low, rectangular seat and a straight, upright backrest. The upholstery is adorned with a bold, black-and-white geometric pattern, giving it a striking visual presence. The seat and backrest are both well-padded, providing comfort for sitting, while the absence of armrests enhances its sleek silhouette. Four slender, straight metal legs support the structure, finished in a dark color that complements the upholstery. The overall form is compact and functional, suitable for urban environments such as parks, plazas, or contemporary public spaces. The materials and construction suggest durability and ease of maintenance for high-traffic areas.
description: A modern chaise with bold black-and-white geometric upholstery, straight metal legs, and a compact, armless design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the seat and backrest, highlighting the geometric upholstery pattern and the straight, armless silhouette.
description_view_1: Left side view revealing the depth of the seat, the upright backrest, and the slender profile of the metal legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the chaise and the continuation of the geometric pattern, with both back legs visible.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the seat depth, backrest height, and the angled positioning of the legs.
attribute_backrest_design: The backrest is straight, rectangular, and moderately tall, with a flat surface and no visible segmentation or decorative elements beyond the geometric upholstery pattern.
attribute_armrest_presence: No armrests are present on either side.
attribute_leg_style: The legs are straight, slender, and made of metal, finished in a dark color with a simple, functional design and no decorative elements.
attribute_upholstery_pattern: The upholstery features a bold, geometric pattern in black and white, with large, abstract shapes creating a visually dynamic surface.
attribute_cushion_arrangement: There are two main cushions: one forming the seat and one forming the backrest, both attached and integrated into the frame, with no loose or additional cushions visible.
